name: The Law Offices of Philip F. Counce
description_short: Memphis-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings with 51 years of combined attorney experience and free consultations.
description_long:
The Law Offices of Philip F. Counce Jr. is a bankruptcy-focused law firm located in Memphis, Tennessee, operating at 3333 Poplar Avenue. The firm has established itself as a dedicated bankruptcy practice with significant cumulative experience among its attorneys, averaging 51 years of combined licensing and practice. The firm maintains an active online presence and accepts new clients through free initial consultations, with both weekday scheduling and toll-free contact options available.

The Law Offices of Philip F. Counce Jr. provides bankruptcy legal representation for consumers filing Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcies. The firm offers free consultations to prospective clients, guidance through the bankruptcy filing process, court representation, and case management. Their service model emphasizes accessibility, with multiple phone lines (local and toll-free), a dedicated website for bankruptcy assistance (willbankruptcyhelp.com), and scheduled appointment availability for consultations and ongoing representation.

The firm distinguishes itself through direct attorney involvement in client matters, with Philip F. Counce Jr. and other named attorneys handling cases personally rather than exclusively through paralegals. Client testimonials highlight responsiveness to phone calls, professional demeanor, and detailed guidance through the bankruptcy process. The firm advertises its approachability and commitment to walking clients through each step of their bankruptcy case.

However, the firm's public reviews are mixed, with a 4.0 out of 5 average across four reviews, but including one substantive negative review (25% one-star rating) citing rudeness, lack of pre-court consultation, unresponsiveness to client questions, and concerns about overbooking cases. The critical review alleges the attorney prioritizes volume over client service. Potential clients should weigh positive testimonials against this documented complaint and consider a consultation to assess personal compatibility and communication style.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Free initial consultations available for bankruptcy cases
51 years of combined attorney experience across the firm
Multiple contact methods including toll-free number (866-923-1788) for accessibility
Dedicated bankruptcy website (willbankruptcyhelp.com) with case information resources
Verified client testimonials praising responsiveness to phone calls and prompt communication
Direct attorney involvement in cases rather than solely paralegal-based service
Weekday appointment availability for in-person consultations
cons: Mixed online reviews with one verified client reporting attorney was rude, arrogant, and dismissive before court appearance
Allegation in negative review of overbooking cases and prioritizing fee collection over client service quality
One documented complaint that attorney did not meet with client before court date despite being hired for representation
Limited public information about fee structure, payment plans, or cost estimates for bankruptcy filing services
Only 4 total customer reviews available on directory listing, providing limited feedback sample size
best_for: Memphis-area consumers filing Chapter 7 bankruptcy seeking local attorney representation
Debtors pursuing Chapter 13 repayment plan bankruptcies who prefer established local counsel
Individuals comfortable with established law firm structure and wanting attorney-directed case management
Prospective bankruptcy clients seeking free initial consultations to discuss filing options
diagnosis: The Law Offices of Philip F. Counce Jr. is best for Memphis-area consumers seeking experienced local bankruptcy counsel for Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 filings who value established firm credentials and attorney-level service. The primary caveat is the mixed client feedback, particularly one substantive complaint about attorney responsiveness and demeanor, suggesting prospective clients should carefully evaluate personal compatibility during the free consultation before committing to representation.
services: Bankruptcy case guidance and process navigation
Client communication and phone support during bankruptcy cases
Weekday in-person appointments for bankruptcy matters
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
